summary: The PoC successfully verified that the multipart form-data parser in n8n webhooks is configured without critical resource limits (maxTotalFileSize, maxFieldsSize, maxFields, maxFiles), confirming the denial-of-service vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: The verification script should confirm that the formidable parser configuration in webhook-form-data.ts is missing the four critical limits that prevent resource exhaustion attacks.
actual_observed_result: The verification script confirmed that all four critical limits (maxTotalFileSize, maxFieldsSize, maxFields, maxFiles) are absent from the formidable configuration in webhook-form-data.ts, validating the vulnerability.
verification_method: Static code analysis of webhook-form-data.ts checking for the presence of security-critical formidable options. The absence of these options confirms the vulnerability exists.
evidence_produced_by_run: maxTotalFileSize is NOT set in formidable configuration
maxFieldsSize is NOT set in formidable configuration
maxFields is NOT set in formidable configuration
maxFiles is NOT set in formidable configuration
Vulnerability confirmed: Multipart parser allows unbounded resource consumption

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
reproduction.pinned_commit: HEAD of n8n repository at time of audit
